questions.q: On which date is Republic Day celebrated in India?
questions.explanation: Republic Day is celebrated annually on 26 January.

questions.q: Republic Day marks the day on which which document came into force?
questions.explanation: Republic Day commemorates the coming into force of the Constitution of India.

questions.q: In which year did the first Republic Day take place?
questions.explanation: The Constitution came into force on 26 January 1950, marking the first Republic Day.

questions.q: The Republic Day ceremonial parade is held in which city?
questions.explanation: The main Republic Day parade is held in New Delhi.

questions.q: Becoming a republic in 1950 meant India would have an elected what?
questions.explanation: Becoming a republic meant India had an elected head of state, the President, in place of a monarch.

questions.q: The date of 26 January was chosen to honour a declaration made in 1930. What was that declaration?
questions.explanation: 26 January was chosen to honour the 1930 Purna Swaraj (complete independence) declaration.

questions.q: Republic Day is one of India's three national holidays, the others being Independence Day and which day?
questions.explanation: India's three national holidays are Republic Day, Independence Day and Gandhi Jayanti.

questions.q: Who takes the salute at the main Republic Day parade in New Delhi?
options: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: The President of India, as the head of state, takes the salute at the Republic Day parade.

questions.q: Republic Day celebrations in New Delhi traditionally feature a parade along which route?
questions.explanation: The Republic Day parade proceeds along the Kartavya Path, formerly known as Rajpath.

questions.q: On the first Republic Day, who was sworn in as the first President of India?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad took office as the first President of India on 26 January 1950.

questions.q: The 1930 declaration that inspired the choice of 26 January was adopted at a session of the Indian National Congress in which city?
questions.explanation: The Purna Swaraj resolution was adopted at the 1929 Lahore session, and the pledge was observed on 26 January 1930.

questions.q: Before 26 January 1950, what was India's constitutional status under the existing arrangement?
questions.explanation: From 1947 until 1950, India was a dominion before becoming a republic.

questions.q: The Republic Day celebrations in New Delhi traditionally conclude a few days later with which ceremony?
options: The state of the union address
questions.explanation: The Republic Day festivities formally conclude with the Beating Retreat ceremony, traditionally on 29 January.

questions.q: It is customary for a foreign head of state or government to be invited to the Republic Day parade in what capacity?
questions.explanation: A foreign dignitary is traditionally invited as the Chief Guest at the Republic Day parade.

questions.q: On the first Republic Day in 1950, the first President took the oath at which building in New Delhi?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad took the oath at the Durbar Hall of Government House, later renamed Rashtrapati Bhavan.

questions.q: The Constitution that came into force on Republic Day had been adopted by the Constituent Assembly on which earlier date?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly adopted the Constitution on 26 November 1949, before it came into force on 26 January 1950.

questions.q: Gallantry and civilian honours announced around Republic Day include which of the following series of awards?
questions.explanation: The Padma awards are conventionally announced on the eve of Republic Day each year.

questions.q: With the transition to a republic in 1950, the British monarch ceased to be the constitutional head of the Indian state, replaced by which office?
questions.explanation: On becoming a republic, the office of President of India replaced the Crown as head of state.

questions.q: The Republic Day parade typically features tableaux representing which of the following?
options: States, union territories and government departments
questions.explanation: The parade includes colourful tableaux from states, union territories and various government ministries and departments.

questions.q: Which national symbol is ceremonially honoured during the Republic Day parade?
questions.explanation: The national flag is hoisted and honoured during the Republic Day ceremonies.

questions.q: The last Governor-General of India, who held office until the republic was established, was which of the following?
questions.explanation: C. Rajagopalachari was the last Governor-General of India before the office was abolished on 26 January 1950.

questions.q: The first Republic Day parade in 1950 was held at which venue, before the parade settled at its present route?
questions.explanation: The first Republic Day parade in 1950 was held at the Irwin Stadium, later known as the National Stadium.

questions.q: Who was the Chief Guest at India's first Republic Day celebrations in 1950?
options: President Eisenhower of the United States
questions.explanation: President Sukarno of Indonesia was the Chief Guest at the first Republic Day in 1950.

questions.q: The national anthem played at Republic Day, 'Jana Gana Mana', was adopted as the national anthem by the Constituent Assembly on which date?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly adopted 'Jana Gana Mana' as the national anthem on 24 January 1950.

questions.q: A few articles of the Constitution actually came into operation before 26 January 1950, on which date in 1949?
questions.explanation: Certain provisions of the Constitution came into force on 26 November 1949, the date of adoption, while the rest commenced on 26 January 1950.

questions.q: The date 26 January 1950 was deliberately selected to coincide with the anniversary of which observance first held in 1930?
options: Independence Day, as declared under Purna Swaraj
questions.explanation: 26 January had been observed as Independence Day since 1930 following the Purna Swaraj declaration, so it was chosen for the republic.

questions.q: On its first Republic Day, India became a sovereign republic while choosing to remain a member of which international association?
questions.explanation: India became a republic in 1950 while remaining a member of the Commonwealth of Nations.

questions.q: The Beating Retreat ceremony that concludes Republic Day celebrations is traditionally held at which location in New Delhi?
questions.explanation: The Beating Retreat ceremony is traditionally held at Vijay Chowk in New Delhi.

questions.q: On the first Republic Day, the new head of state delivered an address; in what language tradition is the President's address customarily issued to the nation?
questions.explanation: The President's address to the nation is customarily issued in both Hindi and English.

questions.q: The transition on Republic Day 1950 also led to the renaming of which residence as the home of the new head of state?
options: Teen Murti House became Rashtrapati Bhavan
questions.explanation: The former Viceroy's House (Government House) became Rashtrapati Bhavan, the residence of the President.
